results.notes: Mitsubishi 3000GT twin. Base model has 15" wheels.
R/T Turbo (Twin Turbo) has 17x7.5 wheels. Some sources show 17x8.5. 255/50R17 also OEM option.
5x5.5 inch (5x139.7mm) bolt pattern. Light duty van.
5x5.5 inch bolt pattern. Larger hub bore than typical Chrysler cars.
Same bolt pattern as B250. Heavy duty version.
New naming scheme for 1994+. 5x5.5 inch bolt pattern.
5-lug version. Some HD suspension versions have 8-lug pattern.
8x6.5 inch bolt pattern (8x165.1mm). Heavy duty 1-ton version.
Based on Mitsubishi Eclipse platform. First year.
Continuation of previous years. First generation ended 2000.
C-body platform. Same as Chrysler Dynasty.
Based on Eagle Premier. Renault-derived platform. 5x4.25 inch bolt pattern.
Final year of production. GTC convertible only.
Fifth Avenue trim available with 15" wheels.
New LH platform. Major redesign. 5x4.5 inch bolt pattern.
Final year of production. Name dropped after 1996.
Same as Dodge Dynasty. C-body platform.
Y-body platform. Based on Dynasty/New Yorker.
3rd generation. New NS platform. Bolt pattern changed to 5x4.5 inch.

platformNotes.Stealth: Mitsubishi 3000GT twin. Performance trims had larger wheels. R/T Turbo has 17x8.5 with 245/45R17.
platformNotes.Monaco: Eagle Premier twin. Renault/AMC-derived platform with 5x108 bolt pattern (unusual for Chrysler).
platformNotes.Colt: Mitsubishi Mirage twin. 4x100 bolt pattern, common with Japanese subcompacts.
platformNotes.NewYorker: Platform changed from C-body (5x100) to LH-body (5x114.3) in 1994.
platformNotes.Voyager: Platform changed from AS (5x100) to NS (5x114.3) in 1996.
platformNotes.RamVan: B3500 1-ton vans use 8x165.1 (8x6.5") while lighter models use 5x139.7 (5x5.5").
